label: 2301 Sense of Congress

amendments: Short Title, , , provided that:Pub. L. 116–92, div. F, title LXXII, § 7201133 Stat. 2262Dec. 20, 2019“This title [enacting this chapter and amending , Foreign Relations and Intercourse] may be cited as the ‘Fentanyl Sanctions Act’.”section 2291h of Title 22

children.text: the United States should apply economic and other financial sanctions to foreign traffickers of illicit opioids to protect the national security, foreign policy, and economy of the United States and the health of the people of the United States;

it is imperative that the People’s Republic of China follow through on full implementation of the new regulations, adopted , to treat all fentanyl analogues as controlled substances under the laws of the People’s Republic of China, including by devoting sufficient resources for implementation and strict enforcement of the new regulations; andMay 1, 2019
the effective enforcement of the new regulations should result in diminished trafficking of illicit fentanyl originating from the People’s Republic of China into the United States.
